patient_profile.owner_concerns: Cat is eating less, seems more tired.

hematology_and_chemistry: Serum chemistry panel: BUN, creatinine, SDMA, electrolytes, glucose, total protein, albumin, ALP, ALT, bilirubin
urinalysis: Sediment examination for crystals or bacteria
endocrine_tests: Feline thyroid panel (TSH, fT4) if weight loss >10% or hyperthyroid signs appear
Serum insulin and glucose if diabetes suspected
imaging: Abdominal ultrasound: kidney size/echogenicity, liver parenchyma, bladder wall, ureters

interpretation_guidelines.CKD_progression: Elevated creatinine/SDMA with low SG and proteinuria; stage 3–4 CKD likely.
interpretation_guidelines.Diabetes_mellitus: Hyperglycemia >200 mg/dL fasting or random, glucosuria without ketonuria.
interpretation_guidelines.Hyperthyroidism: High fT4 with normal/low TSH; may see weight loss despite appetite.
interpretation_guidelines.Hepatic_disease: Elevated ALP/ALT and bilirubin; ultrasound changes.

CKD_stage_3_to_4: Adjust renal diet: reduce protein to 0.2–0.25 g/kg, phosphorus <0.5 mg/kg, maintain potassium.
Phosphate binder if serum phosphate >6 mg/dL.
Fluid therapy: subcutaneous fluids BID if dehydration or azotemia.
Blood pressure control: ACE inhibitor (e.g., enalapril) if hypertension detected.
Diabetes_mellitus: Initiate insulin protocol (e.g., glargine SC BID).
Monitor blood glucose daily; adjust diet to low glycemic index.
Owner education on injection technique and hypoglycemia signs.
Hyperthyroidism: Recheck thyroid panel in 4 weeks; titrate dose.
Monitor for adverse effects (e.g., GI upset).

when_to_seek_care: Sudden increase in water consumption or urination >10% of baseline.
Vomiting, diarrhea, or lethargy lasting >24 hrs.

follow_up_schedule.initial_follow_up: 4–6 weeks after starting treatment to reassess clinical signs and repeat CBC/chemistry.
follow_up_schedule.subsequent_visits: Every 3 months for stable CKD; more frequent if lab values worsen or new symptoms arise.
